summary_text: Current `x-auto-post-guard` already covers quote URL placement and basic delete/repost flow, but it is missing hard gates for evidence quality and claim-source fit. I’d promote these clauses: 1. **Medical Metric Evidence Gate** (new non-negotiable rule) `If Body/Body JA contains clinical-effect metrics (%, x倍, risk reduction, QOL improvement, trust uplift), require a primary-source URL that directly supports that exact metric. Otherwise remove the metric before approval/post.` Risk if omitted: fabricated or weakly-supported medical claims pass to production and create credibility/compliance exposure. 2. **Journal-Name-Only Ban** (new non-negotiable rule) `References like "JAMA 2025", "Nature Medicine study", etc. are invalid unless traceable via DOI/PMID/full article URL in source metadata.` Risk if omitted: authority-laundering (prestige journal name without verifiability), high reputational risk. 3. **Claim-Source Alignment Check** (new mandatory preflight item) `Before publish, state one-line mapping: "main claim" -> "which source URL proves it". If mapping fails, clear Source URLs/Reply URLs or rewrite claim.` Risk if omitted: mismatched citations (correct-looking link, wrong context), exactly the failure mode you just saw. 4. **Reply Citation Suitability Rule** (quote/reply expansion) `For non-quote posts, do not auto-attach source reply unless the source is context-fit for the post’s central claim. Prefer no reply over weak/misaligned reply.` Risk if omitted: irrelevant citations undercut post quality even when main body is good. 5. **Manual Repost After Deletion Protocol** (expand delete/repost section) `When canonical tweet was manually deleted: restore row to approved, clear stale Tweet ID/Posted At, repost once, then remove stale reply artifacts from previous attempts and re-sync Notion + posted log.` Risk if omitted: duplicate-block drift, stale IDs, orphaned replies, inconsistent SoT across queue/Notion/logs. 6. **Posted Metadata Hygiene Rule** (new post-publish verification rule) `After repost or citation correction, verify and reconcile: Status, Tweet ID, Posted At, Source URLs, Reply URLs in both Notion and posted history.` Risk if omitted: repeated operational regressions and future incorrect auto-reply behavior. 7. **Escalation Rule for Medical Claims** (new review threshold) `If claim includes patient outcomes or safety impact and evidence is ambiguous, downgrade to structure/process framing (no quantitative effect claim) unless explicit source-grade confirmation is available.` Risk if omitted: overclaiming in high-stakes domain; avoidable trust and policy risk. If you want, I can convert these into insertion-ready text blocks mapped to exact sections in `SKILL.md` (`Non-Negotiable`, `Mandatory Preflight`, `Delete/Repost Recovery`, `Minimal Operating Pattern`).
